generatedSentence: The unlucky wayfarer, living beneath the miasmic overhang of a forgotten city, often felt like the ugliest relic. Their tattered davenport offered little comfort, its surface a blank canvas of neglect. A single, tarnished heirloom spork was their only utensil for a meal of cold hamburger and stale jalapenos. They longed for the euphony of viola and violins, a stark contrast to their solitary existence. An undying threat from jihadis and ancient drakes with their dwarves sought a diadem within a fractal labyrinth. Feeling weak and a washout, courage had to bolster them. Their wand, with an optical lens, could optimize calculations. They began to factorize geometry, planning to funnel foes via the deep foundation. Deep within, an opaline light revealed a macabre scene: a mandorla of swirling mycelium clinging to ancient jewelry. The jovian cultists, in their ceremonial outfit, moved with an elliptic rhythm, their silhouette a menacing jigsaw. As the hero reached the equator of their ritual, they prepared to sail forth. Their wand would soon eject a powerful blast, reflecting the last dewdrop of courage, impacting the slow snail-like cultists. The battle resolved, but unfortunately, cult ubiquity remained. Yet, a meaningful change occurred. The hero understood heuristic cosmic energy, seeing filigree. Holograms of victory spread, a vanguard against vaporware promises. They felt a desire to boogie as a bison roamed under windy skies. An earthworm sensed the shift, hypothec sealed in vacuum of sweets.
panelSentences: The unlucky wayfarer, living beneath the miasmic overhang of a forgotten city, often felt like the ugliest relic. Their tattered davenport offered little comfort, its surface a blank canvas of neglect. A single, tarnished heirloom spork was their only utensil for a meal of cold hamburger and stale jalapenos. They longed for the euphony of viola and violins, a stark contrast to their solitary existence.

An undying threat from jihadis and ancient drakes with their dwarves sought a diadem within a fractal labyrinth. Feeling weak and a washout, courage had to bolster them. Their wand, with an optical lens, could optimize calculations. They began to factorize geometry, planning to funnel foes via the deep foundation.

Deep within, an opaline light revealed a macabre scene: a mandorla of swirling mycelium clinging to ancient jewelry. The jovian cultists, in their ceremonial outfit, moved with an elliptic rhythm, their silhouette a menacing jigsaw. As the hero reached the equator of their ritual, they prepared to sail forth. Their wand would soon eject a powerful blast, reflecting the last dewdrop of courage, impacting the slow snail-like cultists.

The battle resolved, but unfortunately, cult ubiquity remained. Yet, a meaningful change occurred. The hero understood heuristic cosmic energy, seeing filigree. Holograms of victory spread, a vanguard against vaporware promises. They felt a desire to boogie as a bison roamed under windy skies. An earthworm sensed the shift, hypothec sealed in vacuum of sweets.
rawPanelSentences: The **unlucky** **wayfarer**, living **beneath** the **miasmic** **overhang** of a forgotten city, often felt like the **ugliest** relic. Their tattered **davenport** offered little comfort, its surface a **blank** canvas of neglect. A single, tarnished **heirloom** **spork** was their only utensil for a meal of cold **hamburger** and stale **jalapenos**. They longed for the **euphony** of **viola** and **violins**, a stark contrast to their solitary existence.

An **undying** threat from **jihadis** and ancient **drakes** with their **dwarves** sought a **diadem** within a **fractal** labyrinth. Feeling **weak** and a **washout**, courage had to **bolster** them. Their **wand**, with an **optical** lens, could **optimize** calculations. They began to **factorize** geometry, planning to **funnel** foes via the deep **foundation**.

Deep within, an **opaline** light revealed a **macabre** scene: a **mandorla** of swirling **mycelium** clinging to ancient **jewelry**. The **jovian** cultists, in their ceremonial **outfit**, moved with an **elliptic** rhythm, their **silhouette** a menacing **jigsaw**. As the hero reached the **equator** of their ritual, they prepared to **sail** forth. Their wand would soon **eject** a powerful blast, reflecting the last **dewdrop** of courage, impacting the slow **snail**-like cultists.

The battle resolved, but **unfortunately**, cult **ubiquity** remained. Yet, a **meaningful** change occurred. The hero understood **heuristic** cosmic energy, seeing **filigree**. **Holograms** of victory spread, a **vanguard** against **vaporware** promises. They felt a desire to **boogie** as a **bison** roamed under **windy** skies. An **earthworm** sensed the shift, **hypothec** sealed in **vacuum** of **sweets**.
imagePrompt: A professional 4-panel comic book page, 2x2 grid, sequential art, neo-noir sci-fi comic book style, thick white gutters, clean black borders. The Wayfarer, wearing a patched, deep-violet duster coat and a glowing, intricate gauntlet on their right hand. TOP-LEFT PANEL: The Wayfarer sits on a dilapidated davenport, gazing at a tarnished spork and a humble meal. Close-up shot, pensive mood. TOP-RIGHT PANEL: The Wayfarer standing before a looming, fractal cave entrance, holding a glowing wand, with faint silhouettes of drakes and dwarves within. Wide shot, establishing awe and danger. BOTTOM-LEFT PANEL: The Wayfarer in dynamic action, ejecting an energy blast from their gauntlet towards menacing, cultist silhouettes illuminated by opaline light. Low angle shot, emphasizing power. BOTTOM-RIGHT PANEL: The Wayfarer looking out over a desolate landscape, holograms of victory floating in the windy air, a bison grazing in the distance. Medium shot, conveying peace and contemplation. cel-shading, bold ink outlines, cinematic lighting, NO text, NO speech bubbles, NO dialogue.

definitions.definition: Displeasing to the eye; not aesthetically pleasing.
Displeasing to the ear or some other sense.
Offensive to one's sensibilities or morality.
definitions.example: He played an ugly trick on us.

definitions.definition: Ill-natured; crossgrained; quarrelsome.
definitions.example: an ugly temper; to feel ugly

definitions.definition: Unpleasant; disagreeable; likely to cause trouble or loss.
definitions.example: With all this competition, expect things to get ugly.

definitions.definition: Unfortunate, marked by misfortune.
Inauspicious.
Having ill luck.
Bringing ill luck.
Happening through bad luck, or because of some unfortunate event.
Used (as a parenthetical word) to express disappointment, compassion, sorrow, regret or grief.
The state or quality of being, or appearing to be, everywhere at once; actual or perceived omnipresence.
Anything that is ubiquitous within a specified area.
To come back to life after having died.
To become undead.
Permanent; never-ending; infinite
definitions.example: Just doesn't understand

definitions.definition: A jihadist.
A cultivar of hot chili pepper, Capsicum annuum.
A saw with fine teeth and a narrow blade which can cut curves in wood or metal.
A jigsaw puzzle.
To cut something using a jigsaw.
Collectively, personal ornamentation such as rings, necklaces, brooches and bracelets, made of precious metals and sometimes set with gemstones.
definitions.example: She had more jewellery ornamented about her than any three ladies needed.

definitions.definition: Pertaining to the planet Jupiter.
Pertaining to the Roman god Jove or Jupiter (the counterpart of the Greek god Zeus); Jove-like; befitting Jupiter.
Of, or relating to sight; visual.
definitions.example: Strabismus is an optical defect.

definitions.definition: Designed to assist or enhance sight
definitions.example: A microscope is an optical instrument.

definitions.definition: Of, or relating to optics.
definitions.example: Refraction is an optical effect.

definitions.definition: Of, or relating to visible light.
definitions.example: Optical telescopes don't work when it is cloudy.

definitions.definition: Incorporating light-sensitive devices.
definitions.example: An optical switch opens the door automatically.

definitions.definition: (originally intransitive) To act optimistically or as an optimist.
To make (something) optimal.
To make (something) more efficient, such as a computer program.
To become optimal.
A set of clothing (with accessories).
definitions.example: She wore a fashionable outfit with matching purse and shoes.

definitions.definition: Gear consisting of a set of articles or tools for a specified purpose.
Any cohesive group of people; a unit; such as a military company.
A business or firm.
definitions.example: Should we buy it here, or do you think the outfit across town will have a better deal?

definitions.definition: A sports team.
An outlier-sensitive fit.
To provide with, usually for a specific purpose.
definitions.example: The expedition was outfitted with proper clothing, food, and other necessities.

definitions.definition: The volume that tips the balance between the demand and the supply toward demand lagging supply.
That portion of the roof structure that extends beyond the exterior walls of a building.
A fatty roll of pubis flab that hangs over one's genitals; a FUPA.
Anything that overhangs or protrudes over its base, such as a wave immediately before breaking, or a protruding cliff or rock wall.
To hang over (something).
To impend.
A clear to white liquid secreted by sea hares (genus Aplysia) that becomes viscous upon contact with water
definitions.example: The attack on a sea hare by a spiny lobster causes release of the whitish opaline, followed the co-release of both opaline and ink.

definitions.definition: Any of several minerals resembling opal, including a variety of yellow chalcedony
Opal glass
Resembling opal in its iridescence; having a milky white iridescence.
Representing or personifying death.
Obsessed with death or the gruesome.
Ghastly, shocking, terrifying.
The vegetative part of any fungus, consisting of a mass of branching, threadlike hyphae, often underground.
Having meaning, significant.
definitions.example: I think we made a meaningful contribution to this project today.

definitions.definition: A vesica piscis-shaped aureola that surrounds the figures of Christ and the Virgin Mary in traditional Christian art.
Filled with miasma; containing noxious vapors.
Composed of or resembling vapors.

Any member of a race of beings from (especially Scandinavian and other Germanic) folklore, usually depicted as having some sort of supernatural powers and being skilled in crafting and metalworking, often as short with long beards, and sometimes as clashing with elves.
A person of short stature, often one whose limbs are disproportionately small in relation to the body as compared with normal adults, usually as the result of a genetic condition.
An animal, plant or other thing much smaller than the usual of its sort.
definitions.example: dwarf honeysuckle

definitions.definition: A star of relatively small size.
An ornamental headband worn as a badge of royalty.
A crown.
Regal power; sovereignty; empire—considered as symbolized by the crown.
An arch rising from the rim of a crown (rarely also of a coronet), and uniting with others over its centre.
To adorn with a diadem; to crown.
A male duck.
A mayfly used as fishing bait.
A dragon.
A small piece of artillery.
A fiery meteor.
A beaked galley, or Viking warship.
A droplet of water formed as dew.
(1800s) A slow pitch.
A large sofa, especially a formal one.
A writing desk.
To create a list of the factors of.
To divide an expression into a list of items that, when multiplied together, will produce the original quantity.
To warn not to pay or give up goods.
To attach the effects of a debtor in the hands of a third party.

A utensil in the shape of an inverted hollow cone terminating in a narrow pipe, for channeling liquids or granular material; typically used when transferring said substances from any container into ones with a significantly smaller opening.
A passage or avenue for a fluid or flowing substance; specifically, a smoke flue or pipe; the chimney of a steamship or the like.
To use a funnel.
To proceed through a narrow gap or passageway akin to a funnel; to condense or narrow.
definitions.example: Expect delays where the traffic funnels down to one lane.

definitions.definition: To channel, direct, or focus (emotions, money, resources, etc.).

definitions.definition: To consume (beer, etc.) rapidly through a funnel, typically as a stunt at a party.
The act of founding, fixing, establishing, or beginning to erect.
definitions.example: The foundation of his institute has been wrought with difficulty.

definitions.definition: That upon which anything is founded; that on which anything stands, and by which it is supported; the lowest and supporting layer of a superstructure; underbuilding.
The result of the work to begin something; that which stabilizes and allows an enterprise or system to develop.
In solitaire or patience games, one of the piles of cards that the player attempts to build, usually holding all cards of a suit in ascending order.
The lowest and supporting part or member of a wall, including the base course and footing courses; in a frame house, the whole substructure of masonry.

definitions.definition: A donation or legacy appropriated to support a charitable institution, and constituting a permanent fund; endowment.
That which is founded, or established by endowment; an endowed institution or charity.
definitions.example: The Wikimedia Foundation, Inc. is the parent organization of the Wiktionary collaborative project.

definitions.definition: Cosmetic cream roughly skin-colored, designed to make the face appear uniform in color and texture.
A basis for social bodies or intellectual disciplines.
A mathematical set that has a non-integer and constant Hausdorff dimension; a geometric figure that is self-similar at all scales.

Having a broken part

A design resembling such intricate ornamentation.

An illustrated outline filled in with a solid color(s), usually only black, and intended to represent the shape of an object without revealing any other visual details; a similar appearance produced when the object being viewed is situated in relative darkness with brighter lighting behind it; a profile portrait in black, such as a shadow appears to be.

Any of very many animals (either hermaphroditic or nonhermaphroditic), of the class Gastropoda, having a coiled shell.

A spiral cam, or a flat piece of metal of spirally curved outline, used for giving motion to, or changing the position of, another part, as the hammer tail of a striking clock.

The pod of the snail clover.
To move or travel very slowly.

definitions.definition: A piece of fabric attached to a boat and arranged such that it causes the wind to drive the boat along. The sail may be attached to the boat via a combination of mast, spars and ropes.

A trip in a boat, especially a sailboat.

A tower-like structure found on the dorsal (topside) surface of submarines.
The floating organ of siphonophores, such as the Portuguese man-of-war.

Drugs

An old crop variety that has been passed down through generations of farmers by seed saving and cultivation, in contrast to modern cultivars used in large-scale agriculture.
A heuristic method.
The art of applying heuristic methods.
A technique designed for solving a problem when classic methods are too slow or fail to find any exact solution.
(of an approach to problem solving, learning, or discovery) That employs a practical method not guaranteed to be optimal or perfect; not following or derived from any theory.
(of a method or algorithm) That solves a problem more quickly but is not certain to arrive at an optimal solution.
(of an argument) That reasons from the value of a method or principle that has been shown by experimental investigation to be a useful aid in learning, discovery and problem-solving.
A three-dimensional image of an object created by holography.
In Scotland, a landlord's right over the stocking (cattle, implements, etc.), and crops of his tenant, as security for payment of rent.
Everything; the whole lot.

Ground beef, especially that intended to be made into hamburgers.
(somewhat crude) An animal or human, or the flesh thereof, that has been badly injured as a result of an accident or conflict.

An organ stop having a similar tone.

A berimbau viola, the smallest member of the berimbau used in capoeira music.

A musical four-string instrument, generally played with a bow or by plucking the string, with the pitch set by pressing the strings at the appropriate place with the fingers; also any instrument of the violin family.
A violinist.
definitions.example: The first violin often plays the lead melody lines in a string quartet.

definitions.definition: A large cushion or pillow.
A pad, quilt, or anything used to hinder pressure, support part of the body, or make a bandage sit easy upon a wounded part; a compress.
A small spacer located on top of the axle of horse-drawn wagons that gives the front wheels enough clearance to turn.
A short, horizontal structural timber between a post and a beam for enlarging the bearing area of the post and/or reducing the span of the beam.

A piece of solid or semisolid mucus in or removed from the nostril cavity.
A black person.
Dancing usually prominently exhibiting movements of the buttocks.
(skydiving) A large, organised skydiving event.
To dance a boogie.
To move, walk, leave, exit.

definitions.definition: Below or underneath.
Below.
In a position that is lower in rank, dignity, etc.
Covered up or concealed by something.
A wild ox, Bison bonasus.
A similar North American animal, Bison bison.
A small French coin, originally of silver, afterwards of copper, worth 5 deniers; also a silver coin of Henry V current in the parts of France then held by the English, worth about 8 pence .
A nonplus .
The white spot in the centre of a target; hence the object to which anything is directed or aimed, the range of such aim .
A lot by which nothing is gained; a ticket in a lottery on which no prize is indicated .
An empty space; a void, for example on a paper .

(now chiefly U.S.) A document, paper, or form with spaces left blank to be filled up at the pleasure of the person to whom it is given (e.g. a blank charter, ballot, form, contract, etc.), or as the event may determine; a blank form .
Blank verse .
A piece of metal (such as a coin, screw, nuts), cut and shaped to the required size of the thing to be made, and ready for the finishing operations; (coining) the disc of metal before stamping .
A vacant space, place, or period; a void .
The 1 / 230400 of a grain .
An empty space in one's memory; a forgotten item or memory .
A dash written in place of an omitted letter or word
The space character; the character resulting from pressing the space-bar on a keyboard.
A domino without points on one or both of its divisions.
definitions.example: the double blank

definitions.definition: Short for blank cartridge. .
(in the expression ‘shooting blanks’) An ineffective effort which achieves nothing .
To make void; to erase.
definitions.example: I blanked out my previous entry.

definitions.definition: To ignore (a person) deliberately.
definitions.example: She blanked me for no reason.

definitions.definition: To prevent from scoring, for example in a sporting event.
definitions.example: England blanks Wales to advance to the final.

definitions.definition: To become blank.
To be temporarily unable to remember.
definitions.example: I'm blanking on her name right now.

definitions.definition: White or pale; without colour.
Free from writing, printing, or marks; having an empty space to be filled in
definitions.example: a blank ballot

definitions.definition: Scoreless; without any goals or points.
Lacking characteristics which give variety; uniform.
definitions.example: a blank desert; a blank wall; blank unconsciousness

definitions.definition: Absolute; downright; sheer.
definitions.example: There was a look of blank terror on his face.

definitions.definition: Without expression.
definitions.example: Failing to understand the question, he gave me a blank stare.

definitions.definition: Utterly confounded or discomfited.
Empty; void; without result; fruitless.
definitions.example: a blank day

definitions.definition: Devoid of thoughts, memory, or inspiration.
definitions.example: The shock left his memory blank.

definitions.definition: Of ammunition: having propellant but no bullets; unbulleted.
definitions.example: The recruits were issued with blank rounds for a training exercise.

definitions.definition: Lacking in force (usually strength) or ability.
definitions.example: The child was too weak to move the boulder.

definitions.definition: Unable to sustain a great weight, pressure, or strain.
definitions.example: a weak timber; a weak rope

definitions.definition: Unable to withstand temptation, urgency, persuasion, etc.; easily impressed, moved, or overcome; accessible; vulnerable.
definitions.example: weak resolutions; weak virtue

definitions.definition: Dilute, lacking in taste or potency.
definitions.example: We were served stale bread and weak tea.

definitions.definition: (grammar) Displaying a particular kind of inflection, including:
That does not ionize completely into anions and cations in a solution.
definitions.example: a weak acid;  a weak base

definitions.definition: One of the four fundamental forces associated with nuclear decay.
Bad or uncool.
definitions.example: This place is weak.

definitions.definition: Having a narrow range of logical consequences; narrowly applicable. (Often contrasted with a strong statement which implies it.)
Resulting from, or indicating, lack of judgment, discernment, or firmness; unwise; hence, foolish.
Not having power to convince; not supported by force of reason or truth; unsustained.
definitions.example: The prosecution advanced a weak case.

definitions.definition: Lacking in vigour or expression.
definitions.example: a weak sentence; a weak style

definitions.definition: Not prevalent or effective, or not felt to be prevalent; not potent; feeble.
Tending towards lower prices.
definitions.example: a weak market; wheat is weak at present

definitions.definition: Lacking contrast.
definitions.example: a weak negative

definitions.definition: A traveller, especially one on foot.
A type of glasses, with pointed ends and rounded bottoms.
An appliance designed to wash something out.
definitions.example: The cistern was fitted with washouts and air-valves.

definitions.definition: The cleaning of matter from a physiological system using a fluid.
definitions.example: a bladder washout

definitions.definition: A period between clinical treatments in which any medication delivered as the first treatment is allowed to wash out of the person before the second treatment begins.
The action whereby falling rainwater clean particles from the air.
A channel produced by the erosion of a relatively soft surface by a sudden gush of water.
A breach in a road or railway caused by flooding.
The cleaning of the inside of a (locomotive) boiler to remove scale (limescale).
A disappointment or total failure; an unsuccessful person.
definitions.example: As an actor, he was a complete washout, so he went back to accounting.

definitions.definition: A sporting fixture or other event that could not be completed because of rain.
The aerodynamic effect of a small twist in the shape of an aircraft wing
A hand-held narrow rod, usually used for pointing or instructing, or as a traditional emblem of authority.
(by extension) An instrument shaped like a wand, such as a curling wand.
A magic wand.
A stick, branch, or stalk, especially of willow.
A card of a particular suit of the minor arcana in tarot, the wands.
To scan (e.g. a passenger at an airport) with a metal detector.
Fart
Accompanied by wind.
definitions.example: It was a long and windy night.

definitions.definition: Unsheltered and open to the wind.
definitions.example: They made love in a windy bus shelter.

definitions.definition: Empty and lacking substance.
definitions.example: They made windy promises they would not keep.

definitions.definition: Long-winded; orally verbose.
Flatulent.
Nervous, frightened.
To compel (a person or persons) to leave.
definitions.example: Andrew was ejected from his apartment for not paying the rent.

definitions.definition: To throw out or remove forcefully.
definitions.example: In other news, a Montreal man was ejected from his car when he was involved in an accident.

definitions.definition: To compel (a sports player) to leave the field because of inappropriate behaviour.
To project oneself from an aircraft.
definitions.example: The pilot lost control of the plane and had to eject.

definitions.definition: To cause (something) to come out of a machine.
definitions.example: Press that button to eject the video tape.

definitions.definition: To come out of a machine.
definitions.example: I can't get this cassette to  eject.

definitions.definition: An inferred object of someone else's consciousness
Of or pertaining to an ellipse.
Of or pertaining to a broad field of mathematics that originates from the problem of calculating arc lengths of an ellipse.
definitions.example: Elliptic curve

definitions.definition: (in combination, of certain functions, equations and operators) That has coefficients satisfying a condition analogous to the condition for the general equation for a conic section to be of an ellipse.
definitions.example: Elliptic operator

definitions.definition: (of leaves) Oval, with a short or no point.
A pronunciation of letters and syllables which is pleasing to the ear.
Pleasant phonetic quality of certain words.
definitions.example: When I hear you speak, I hear beautiful euphony.

definitions.definition: A worm that lives in the ground.
A worm of the family Lumbricidae, or, more generally, of the suborder Lumbricina.
A contemptible person; a groveller.
(often “the Equator”) An imaginary great circle around the Earth, equidistant from the two poles, and dividing earth's surface into the northern and southern hemisphere.
A similar great circle on any sphere, especially on a celestial body, or on other reasonably symmetrical three-dimensional body.
The midline of any generally spherical object, such as a fruit or vegetable, that has identifiable poles.
definitions.example: Slice the onion through the equator.

definitions.definition: The celestial equator.
